title: Diphryllum ovatum
intro: Diphryllum ovatum, also known as Mexican feather fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and ability to grow on other plants. Traditionally, Diphryllum ovatum has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Diphryllum ovatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbalism, Diphryllum ovatum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as salves and poultices to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Diphryllum ovatum is a species of plant in the family Selaginellaceae. This family is a group of vascular plants commonly known as spike mosses or resurrection ferns. The Selaginellaceae family consists of approximately 700 species, many of which are epiphytic or terrestrial plants found in tropical and subtropical regions. Diphryllum ovatum, like other species in the family, is a small, herbaceous plant with simple leaves. The classification of Diphryllum ovatum is further refined by its genus and species within the broader family classification.

properties: Diphryllum ovatum has been used in traditional medicine for its reported therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to possess compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. constituents: Diphryllum ovatum is a species of plant that contains various active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which are known for their antioxidant properties.
